title: What the 'Mother of All Trade Deals' Learned From the India-ASEAN FTA
gist: India and the EU signed a 23-chapter Free Trade Agreement on January 27, 2026 after nearly two decades of stalled talks; the author frames it against the precedent of India's 2009 ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement, the country's first major regional market opening since 1991 liberalization.

insights.claim: India and the European Union signed a landmark Free Trade Agreement on January 27, 2026, dubbed the 'mother of all deals' because it followed nearly two decades of stalled negotiations and comprises a comprehensive package of 23 chapters covering goods, services, digital trade, and sustainability.
insights.speaker: Latha Reddy
insights.so_what: A 23-chapter India-EU FTA spanning goods, services, digital trade, and sustainability is a major market-access event for EU and Indian exporters and investors across those sectors.
insights.locator: This deal was widely described as

insights.claim: The India-EU FTA followed a formal detente in 2022 that relaunched negotiations after roughly two decades of stalling.
insights.speaker: Latha Reddy
insights.so_what: Establishes the negotiation timeline investors can use to gauge how durable and hard-won the deal's terms are likely to be.

insights.claim: The author draws on lived experience negotiating India's first major trade agreement, the 2009 ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA), as the relevant precedent for what to expect from the 'mother of all deals' with the EU.
insights.speaker: Latha Reddy
insights.so_what: Signals that implementation and political friction seen during AITIGA's rollout is the author's benchmark for what could complicate India-EU FTA execution — worth reading the fuller piece for specific parallels.
insights.locator: For those who lived through the negotiation

insights.claim: AITIGA (2009) marked a pivotal shift for India: it was the first time since 1991 liberalization that India opened its markets to a multi-country regional bloc, moving away from its historically protectionist trade policy.
insights.speaker: Latha Reddy
insights.so_what: Contextualizes India's trade liberalization trajectory — a structural data point for assessing how far India has moved from protectionism ahead of the larger EU deal.
insights.quote: fundamentally moving away from its protectionist trade policies
insights.locator: The ASEAN-India deal marked a pivotal shift
